meaning: adj.自然的,自然主义的,博物学的
examples: Further research is needed under rather more naturalistic conditions.
So do the naturalistic paintings on slabs of stone excavated in southern Africa.
He's often hoarse at the end of the day because he performs his dialogue out loud as he writes it. (This may account for its strikingly naturalistic quality.)
Her abstract canvases were naturalistic because they had all the visual magic of natural phenomena.
Under the naturalistic direction of two-time Obie Award winner Leigh Silverman, the atmosphere is informal.
